scenarios.description: children() on new Element with no child nodes returns empty list (childNodeSize()==0)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() returns single child Element (childNodeSize()>0, one Element node only)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("p"); parent.appendElement("span"); // one child
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly that span element at index 0

scenarios.description: children() filters out non-Element nodes among mixed childNodes (presence of TextNode and Element nodes)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendText("txt"); el.appendElement("a"); el.appendText("txt2"); el.appendElement("b");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains only two element children <a> and <b> in order

scenarios.description: children() returns cached list on second call without modification (cachedChildren()!=null)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("ul"); el.appendElement("li"); // call children twice to hit cache
scenarios.expectedResult: second call returns same unmodifiable list instance

scenarios.description: children() invalidates cache on modification then recomputes list (after appendChild)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("ol"); el.appendElement("li"); el.children(); // cached; then append another
scenarios.expectedResult: after appending new child, children() returns updated list of size 2

scenarios.description: children() with multiple (>2) child Elements returns correct order and count
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); for i in 1..5 el.appendElement("p");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements size is 5 and tagName() of all elements is "p" in order
